project: WUI Multi-Plan: Login, Plans List, Task CRUD

tasks.description: Operator pre-check gate: before any code is written, grep whilly_logs/whilly_events.jsonl for distinct plan_id values seen in the last 30 days. Print the count. If the count is <5, the PRD is over-scoped and the operator should consider aborting and shipping only a minimal plan-list surface instead. The task is complete when the operator has reviewed the count and explicitly decided to proceed (or abort). This is a human_loop task — set status to done only after the operator confirms in writing.
acceptance_criteria: Operator has run the jq/grep command and knows the distinct plan_id count for the last 30 days
Operator has made a documented go/no-go decision (proceed with PRD or abort)
test_steps: python3 -c "import json, datetime; cutoff=(datetime.datetime.utcnow()-datetime.timedelta(days=30)).isoformat(); ids=set(); [ids.add(json.loads(l).get('plan_id')) for l in open('whilly_logs/whilly_events.jsonl') if json.loads(l).get('ts','')>=cutoff and json.loads(l).get('plan_id')]; print(f'Distinct plan_ids in last 30 days: {len(ids)} — {ids}')"
Operator reviews count; if <5 considers abort per PRD §1; explicitly sets this task status to done after decision
tasks.prd_requirement: PRD §1 — Baseline measurement gate

tasks.description: Apply Migration 018: create the magic_links and sessions tables with all required indexes. This is the foundational DB migration that all auth code in Phase 1 depends on. Run alembic upgrade head after adding the revision file. Verify tables and indexes exist.
acceptance_criteria: Migration file 018_sessions_and_magic_links.py exists and is a valid Alembic revision
Tables magic_links and sessions exist in the DB after alembic upgrade head
Partial unique index uq_magic_links_active_email exists (WHERE consumed_at IS NULL AND expires_at > now())
Indexes ix_magic_links_email_issued, ix_magic_links_expires_at, ix_sessions_email_active, ix_sessions_expires_at all exist
test_steps: psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='magic_links'" | grep -E 'uq_magic_links_active_email|ix_magic_links'
pytest tests/test_migrations.py -k 'migration_018' -v || echo 'migration smoke test not yet written — manual DB check above is sufficient'
tasks.prd_requirement: Migration 018_sessions_and_magic_links / §6.3

tasks.description: Apply Migrations 019a and 019b: 019a adds archived_at and last_event_at columns to the plans table using ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS plus CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (online, no lock). 019b is an empty reserved revision. Both must be Alembic revisions applied in sequence.
acceptance_criteria: 019a revision exists and adds archived_at timestamptz and last_event_at timestamptz to plans
019a uses ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS and CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (no ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on plans)
019b revision exists and is intentionally empty (placeholder)
alembic upgrade head succeeds; alembic current shows 019b as head
Index ix_plans_active_last_event exists with WHERE archived_at IS NULL
test_steps: psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='plans' AND column_name IN ('archived_at','last_event_at')"
psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT indexname, indexdef FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='plans' AND indexname='ix_plans_active_last_event'"
tasks.prd_requirement: Migration 019a_plans_archived_at / Migration 019b / §6.3 / SC-5.2

tasks.description: Implement whilly/api/auth_tokens.py — pure stdlib module (no FastAPI imports) for magic-link token mint/verify and session-cookie value mint/verify. Mirror the shape of dashboard_token.py: HMAC-signed, opaque, with expiry embedded. Include the reuse pattern logic: a function that determines whether an existing unconsumed link is young enough to be reused (issued_at > now() - ttl/3) versus minting a new token.
acceptance_criteria: auth_tokens.py imports only stdlib: hmac, secrets, time, hashlib (no fastapi, no sqlalchemy)
mint_magic_token(email, ttl_seconds) returns an opaque URL-safe string
verify_magic_token(token, secret) returns (email, issued_at) or raises ValueError on invalid/expired
token_hash(token) returns the SHA-256 hex hash suitable for DB storage
should_reuse_token(issued_at, ttl_seconds) returns True if issued within ttl/3
mint_session_cookie_value(session_id, secret) and verify_session_cookie_value(value, secret) work symmetrically
Unit tests pass without a running DB or FastAPI app
test_steps: python3 -c "from whilly.api.auth_tokens import mint_magic_token, verify_magic_token, token_hash; t=mint_magic_token('x@x.com', 900, 'secret'); print(verify_magic_token(t, 'secret'))"

python3 -c "import ast, pathlib; src=pathlib.Path('whilly/api/auth_tokens.py').read_text(); tree=ast.parse(src); [print(n.names) for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.Import)]; print('no fastapi' if 'fastapi' not in src else 'FAIL: fastapi found')"
python3 -m importlinter --config setup.cfg 2>/dev/null || echo 'importlinter check skipped (WUI-030 task)'
tasks.prd_requirement: FR-A2 / §6.1 auth_tokens.py / Architect F9

tasks.description: Implement whilly/api/sessions.py — DB repository for magic_links and sessions tables. Functions: create_magic_link, find_active_magic_link, consume_magic_link, create_session, verify_session, revoke_session. All use explicit SQL against the tables from Migration 018. No FastAPI imports. The find_active_magic_link function must use the partial index (consumed_at IS NULL AND expires_at > now()) to return the current live link for an email, enabling the reuse pattern.
acceptance_criteria: sessions.py has no FastAPI imports
create_magic_link(conn, token_hash, email, expires_at) inserts a row into magic_links
find_active_magic_link(conn, email) returns the most recent unconsumed unexpired link row (or None)
consume_magic_link(conn, token_hash) sets consumed_at=now() atomically, returns True if row existed
create_session(conn, session_id, email, expires_at) inserts a sessions row
verify_session(conn, session_id) returns session row if not revoked and not expired, else None
revoke_session(conn, session_id) sets revoked_at=now()
test_steps: python3 -c "import inspect, whilly.api.sessions as s; src=inspect.getsource(s); print('no fastapi' if 'fastapi' not in src else 'FAIL')"
tasks.prd_requirement: FR-A2 / §6.1 sessions.py / Architect F7 (reuse pattern)

tasks.description: Implement whilly/api/csrf.py — WhillySessionCSRFMiddleware Starlette middleware. On every state-mutating request (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) whose auth resolved via cookie, verify the Origin header against the csrf_origin_allowlist from whilly.toml [wui] section. Bearer-authenticated and JWT-authenticated requests bypass CSRF. Reject with 403 Forbidden if Origin is absent or not in the allowlist. The middleware must short-circuit before any handler runs.
acceptance_criteria: WhillySessionCSRFMiddleware is a Starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware subclass
GET/HEAD/OPTIONS requests pass through unconditionally
POST/PATCH/DELETE with Authorization: Bearer ... pass through (bearer bypass)
POST/PATCH/DELETE with Cookie: whilly_session=... and valid Origin in allowlist pass through
POST/PATCH/DELETE with Cookie: whilly_session=... and Origin NOT in allowlist return 403 before any handler
POST/PATCH/DELETE with Cookie: whilly_session=... and no Origin header return 403
Allowlist is read from config (list of strings, default includes localhost:8000 and 127.0.0.1:8000)
test_steps: python3 -c "from whilly.api.csrf import WhillySessionCSRFMiddleware; print('import ok')"
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-1.3 / G5 / §6.1 csrf.py / Architect F1

tasks.description: Implement whilly/api/auth_routes.py — FastAPI router with the full login funnel and session management endpoints: GET /login, POST /auth/login, GET /auth/magic, GET /me, POST /auth/logout. Implement cookie helpers set_session_cookie and clear_session_cookie. POST /auth/login implements the reuse pattern (checks for an active link via sessions.py before minting a new one) and writes exactly one auth.magic_link.issued event to whilly_events.jsonl. GET /auth/magic verifies token, marks it consumed, creates a session, sets the whilly_session cookie, and 302-redirects to /. If token is already consumed, renders login_consumed.html.j2. Cookie attributes: HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, Path=/, Secure only when session_cookie_secure=true in config.
acceptance_criteria: GET /login renders login.html.j2 with optional ?email= pre-fill
POST /auth/login with valid email: reuses existing active link if within ttl/3, otherwise mints new; writes exactly one auth.magic_link.issued event; renders login_check_inbox.html.j2
GET /auth/magic with valid unconsumed token: sets whilly_session cookie (HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, Path=/), 302 to /
GET /auth/magic with consumed or expired token: renders login_consumed.html.j2 (200, not redirect)
GET /me with valid session cookie: returns JSON {email, created_at, expires_at}
POST /auth/logout with valid session cookie: revokes session, clears cookie, 204
login.html.j2 includes 'Wrong address? Send again' link to /login?email=<value>
Dev-mode magic link is NOT rendered in login_check_inbox.html.j2 (only in whilly_events.jsonl)
test_steps: curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/auth/login -d 'email=test@test.com' -L | grep -i 'check your inbox\|inbox'

python3 -c "import json; lines=[l for l in open('whilly_logs/whilly_events.jsonl') if 'magic_link.issued' in l]; e=json.loads(lines[-1]); print(e['payload']['link'] if 'link' in e.get('payload',{}) else 'link missing')"
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic A (A1–A5) / FR-A1 FR-A2 FR-A3 FR-A4 FR-A5 / SC-1.1 SC-1.2 SC-2.3 / §6.1 auth_routes.py

tasks.description: Extend whilly/adapters/transport/server.py: (1) Mount WhillySessionCSRFMiddleware before all routers. (2) Mount auth_routes router. (3) Extend the _authenticate_* helpers with the cookie auth path after bearer/JWT — cookie principal carries synthetic scope operator.* only, which cannot satisfy worker-only endpoints. (4) Invert auth chain to bearer → JWT → cookie. (5) Implement the D2 dispatcher on GET /: no-session+no-token redirects to /login; no-session+valid-token renders share-link mode; session redirects to plans list.
acceptance_criteria: WhillySessionCSRFMiddleware is installed in create_app() before route handlers
auth_routes router is mounted (login, /auth/*, /me endpoints reachable)
GET / with no cookie and no ?token= returns 302 to /login
GET / with valid session cookie returns 200 (plans list or redirect to plans)
Bearer token outranks cookie: request with both Authorization: Bearer X and Cookie: whilly_session=Y is authenticated as bearer principal
Cookie principal scope is operator.* and is rejected by /tasks/claim, /workers/register
Existing ?token=<worker-bearer>&plan_id=X flow still works without a session cookie (SC-4.1)
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic A (A6 A7) / SC-1.4 SC-4.1 SC-5.1 / §6.2 server.py / Architect F2

tasks.description: Add repository methods for plan listing and archiving to whilly/adapters/db/repository.py: list_plans(conn, include_archived: bool) — explicit parameter, no default; archive_plan(conn, plan_id); unarchive_plan(conn, plan_id). The list_plans query enforces WHERE archived_at IS NULL when include_archived=False. Implement GET /api/v1/plans endpoint in whilly/api/plans_api.py that calls list_plans and returns PlanPayload list with ETag header per plan. Session-only auth (no bearer).
acceptance_criteria: list_plans(conn, include_archived=False) returns only non-archived plans
list_plans(conn, include_archived=True) returns all plans including archived
No default value on include_archived parameter (must always be explicit)
GET /api/v1/plans with session cookie returns 200 JSON {plans: [...]}
GET /api/v1/plans with bearer token returns 403 (session-only endpoint)
GET /api/v1/plans without any auth returns 401
Each plan in response includes plan_id, name, archived_at, last_event_at (computed), budget_usd, task counts
test_steps: curl -s -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID_TOKEN' http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/plans | python3 -m json.tool | grep plan_id

psql $DATABASE_URL -c "UPDATE plans SET archived_at=now() WHERE plan_id='test-plan'" && curl -s -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID_TOKEN' http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/plans | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([p['plan_id'] for p in d['plans']])" | grep -v test-plan
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic B (B1 B2 B7) / SC-2.1 SC-5.1 / §6.2 repository.py / §6.1 plans_api.py / Architect F5

tasks.description: Extend whilly/api/dashboard.py to accept an auth_context: AuthContext parameter so the rendered HTML can show 'Signed in as <email>' in the header. Move the single-plan dashboard body (Tasks panel, Workers panel, Events SSE, Create Task form, Jira import form) to route GET /plans/<plan_id>. Workers panel becomes read-only — shows online workers (heartbeat ≤ 30s) with a CLI copy-paste snippet below it (D1). The / dispatcher now routes authenticated users to the plans list page, not directly to a single plan.
acceptance_criteria: render_dashboard(request, plan_id, auth_context) accepts auth_context and passes email to template
GET /plans/<plan_id> with valid session renders the single-plan dashboard
GET /plans/<plan_id> with valid ?token=&plan_id= (no session) renders share-link mode banner
Workers panel on /plans/<plan_id> is read-only (no Launch/Stop buttons)
Workers panel shows copy-paste CLI snippet with correct plan_id substitution
Authenticated GET / renders plans list, not a single plan dashboard
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic D (D1 D2 D3) / SC-4.1 SC-4.2 / §6.2 dashboard.py

tasks.description: Update whilly/api/templates/index.html.j2 to implement the plans-list table (Epic B1/B2), header nav (D3), and modal pattern skeleton (D5). Add: <nav> slot rendered only when auth_context.email is set; 'Signed in as <email>' + logout link; '← All plans' breadcrumb on plan pages; sortable HTML <table> for the plans list with columns name/plan_id/task-counts/budget/workers/last_event_at; empty-state 'Create your first plan' CTA; error banner for failed GET /api/v1/plans. The existing single-plan body becomes an {% include %} partial. Add aria-live='polite' status message regions (D4).
acceptance_criteria: <nav> element renders only when auth_context.email is present
Plans table has columns: name, plan_id, pending/in_progress/done/failed counts, budget, workers, last_event_at
Sort by last_event_at DESC by default; column headers have clickable sort links
?q= filter input above table filters by name/plan_id (client-side JS)
Empty state: 'Create your first plan' CTA when plans list is empty
aria-live='polite' regions for success/error feedback on all forms
Single-plan body is extracted to a separate partial (e.g., _plan_dashboard.html.j2) and {% include %}-d
'← All plans' breadcrumb renders on /plans/<plan_id> pages
test_steps: curl -s -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID' http://127.0.0.1:8000/ | grep -E '<table|<nav|All plans|Signed in as'
curl -s -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID' http://127.0.0.1:8000/ | python3 -c "import sys; html=sys.stdin.read(); assert 'aria-live' in html, 'missing aria-live'"
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic B (B1 B2) / Epic D (D3 D4 D5) / SC-4.2 / §6.2 index.html.j2

tasks.description: Add [wui] section to whilly.toml config schema and WhillyConfig.from_env() parsing. Fields: auth_dev_mode (bool, default true), auth_session_ttl (str, default '30d'), auth_magic_link_ttl (str, default '15m'), session_cookie_name (str, default 'whilly_session'), session_cookie_secure (bool, default false), csrf_origin_allowlist (list[str], default ['http://127.0.0.1:8000', 'http://localhost:8000']). Env-var overrides follow WHILLY_ prefix pattern. Log a startup warning when --host 0.0.0.0 and session_cookie_secure=false.
acceptance_criteria: [wui] section parses from whilly.toml into a WuiConfig dataclass
All six fields have correct defaults when section is absent
WHILLY_AUTH_DEV_MODE, WHILLY_AUTH_SESSION_TTL, etc. env vars override toml values
Startup warning logged when host=0.0.0.0 and session_cookie_secure=false
parse_duration(s) helper converts '30d' and '15m' to integer seconds correctly
test_steps: python3 -c "from whilly.config import WhillyConfig; c=WhillyConfig.from_env(); print(c.wui.session_cookie_name, c.wui.csrf_origin_allowlist)"
WHILLY_SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=true python3 -c "from whilly.config import WhillyConfig; c=WhillyConfig.from_env(); assert c.wui.session_cookie_secure"
tasks.prd_requirement: §6.4 Config Schema / G5

tasks.description: Implement login funnel UX refinements: (1) login.html.j2 — email input with 'Wrong address? Send again' link, focus on email field on load, aria labels; (2) login_check_inbox.html.j2 — confirmation page with Send again link (to /login?email=<value>), NO dev-mode magic link shown here; (3) login_consumed.html.j2 — 'This link has already been used or has expired' with [Request a new one] link to /login; (4) A7 JS redirect: when any HTMX call returns 401, navigate to /login?next=<current-path>. All three templates extend base layout.
acceptance_criteria: login.html.j2 focuses the email input on page load (autofocus attribute or JS)
login.html.j2 pre-fills email from ?email= query param
login_check_inbox.html.j2 contains 'Wrong address? Send again' link to /login?email=<submitted-email>
login_check_inbox.html.j2 does NOT contain the magic-link URL in HTML source
login_consumed.html.j2 contains 'Request a new one' link to /login
HTMX 401 response triggers JS redirect to /login?next=<path> (A7)
All three templates pass HTML validation (no unclosed tags)
test_steps: curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8000/auth/magic?token=CONSUMED_TOKEN | grep -i 'already been used\|request a new'
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic A (A2 A3 A5 A7) / SC-1.1 / Frontend F1 F2 / Missing Screens checklist

tasks.description: Write the Migration smoke test (SC-5.2): pytest test that applies migrations 018, 019a, 019b against a testcontainers Postgres instance and verifies (a) all tables and indexes exist, (b) 019a runs without ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock using CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (detectable via pg_stat_activity simulation or statement_timeout), and (c) alembic downgrade works cleanly. This test must pass in CI.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_migrations.py exists with at least test_018_tables_exist, test_019a_additive, test_019b_empty
test_018_tables_exist: verifies magic_links, sessions tables and all 5 indexes
test_019a_additive: verifies archived_at and last_event_at columns exist in plans after upgrade
test_019b_empty: verifies alembic current = 019b after all migrations applied
All migration tests pass: pytest tests/test_migrations.py -v
CI step (or Makefile target) runs migration smoke test
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-5.2 / Migration 018 / Migration 019a / §5 Success Criteria

tasks.description: Write the auth-matrix integration test suite (SC-1.4, SC-5.1): a single pytest file that runs a test server and verifies all 8 cells of the auth matrix: (bearer, cookie, both, neither) × (CRUD endpoint, worker endpoint). Asserts bearer outranks cookie (SC-1.4), cookie is rejected on worker endpoints (SC-5.1), CSRF middleware blocks cookie-auth mutations without matching Origin (SC-1.3), bearer bypasses CSRF (SC-1.3). Must pass against the running server.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_auth_matrix.py has parametrized tests covering all auth × endpoint combinations
bearer_only on CRUD endpoint: 403 (session-only endpoints reject bearer)
cookie_only on CRUD endpoint: 200 (with matching Origin) or 403 (wrong Origin)
bearer_and_cookie on CRUD endpoint: resolves as bearer principal, not cookie
cookie_only on worker endpoint (/tasks/claim): 403
bearer_only on worker endpoint: 200/202
no_auth on any endpoint: 401
pytest tests/test_auth_matrix.py -v passes
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-1.3 SC-1.4 SC-5.1 / Epic A (A6) / Architect F2

tasks.description: Add plan write operations to whilly/adapters/db/repository.py: create_plan(conn, plan_id, name, prd_file, budget_usd) and patch_plan(conn, plan_id, patch_dict, expected_version) with optimistic concurrency (bumps plans.version, returns 412-equivalent ValueError if version moved). Also wire up archive_plan / unarchive_plan if not already done in WUI-009.
acceptance_criteria: create_plan inserts a new plan row with all provided fields; raises ValueError on plan_id conflict (409)
patch_plan accepts dict of changed fields and expected_version; raises StaleVersionError if DB version != expected
patch_plan bumps plans.version on successful update
archive_plan sets archived_at=now(); unarchive_plan sets archived_at=NULL
All methods are covered by unit tests using testcontainers Postgres
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic B (B3 B4) / §6.2 repository.py / Architect F6 (If-Match/ETag)

tasks.description: Implement POST /api/v1/plans and PATCH /api/v1/plans/{plan_id} in whilly/api/plans_api.py. POST creates a plan (session-only auth), returns 201 with PlanPayload and ETag header; returns 409 on plan_id uniqueness conflict with inline error. PATCH accepts {name?, budget_usd?, archived?} plus If-Match header; returns 200 + ETag on success, 412 if version stale. Archive (archived=true) and unarchive (archived=false) go through the same PATCH endpoint. Mount plans_api router in server.py.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/plans with valid payload returns 201 + ETag header
POST /api/v1/plans with duplicate plan_id returns 409 with JSON error including 'plan_id already exists'
POST /api/v1/plans without session cookie returns 401
PATCH /api/v1/plans/{plan_id} with correct If-Match returns 200 + updated ETag
PATCH /api/v1/plans/{plan_id} with stale If-Match returns 412
PATCH /api/v1/plans/{plan_id} with {archived: true} sets archived_at
PATCH /api/v1/plans/{plan_id} with {archived: false} clears archived_at (B4 restore)
PATCH /api/v1/tasks/ on archived plan returns 410 Gone (B6)
test_steps: curl -s -X POST -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"plan_id":"newplan","name":"New"}' http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/plans | python3 -m json.tool
curl -sv -X POST -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"plan_id":"newplan","name":"New"}' http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/plans 2>&1 | grep '409'
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic B (B3 B4 B5 B6) / SC-2.2 / §6.1 plans_api.py / §6.5 API Surface

tasks.description: Add task mutation methods to whilly/adapters/db/repository.py: patch_task(conn, task_id, plan_id, patch_dict, expected_version) with optimistic concurrency (bumps tasks.version, writes task.edited event to whilly_events.jsonl with JSON diff of changed fields) and delete_task(conn, task_id, plan_id, expected_version) (hard delete, writes task.deleted event carrying full pre-deletion task JSON). Both raise StaleVersionError on version mismatch and TaskClaimedError if claimed_by IS NOT NULL.
acceptance_criteria: patch_task updates only supplied fields in patch_dict
patch_task bumps tasks.version on success
patch_task raises StaleVersionError if DB version != expected_version
patch_task raises TaskClaimedError if claimed_by IS NOT NULL
patch_task writes task.edited event with diff of changed fields
delete_task hard-deletes the row
delete_task raises StaleVersionError and TaskClaimedError analogously
delete_task writes task.deleted event with full task JSON in payload
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic C (C2 C3 C4) / SC-3.1 SC-3.2 / §6.2 repository.py

tasks.description: Implement whilly/api/tasks_api_crud.py with PATCH /api/v1/tasks/{task_id} and DELETE /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}. Session-only auth (no bearer). Both endpoints read the If-Match header for expected version, map StaleVersionError to 412 and TaskClaimedError to 409 (with detail including worker_id). PATCH returns 200 + TaskPayload + new ETag. DELETE returns 204. Mount tasks_api_crud router in server.py.
acceptance_criteria: PATCH /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}?plan_id=X with valid If-Match returns 200 + ETag
PATCH /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}?plan_id=X with stale If-Match returns 412 + current ETag in response
PATCH /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}?plan_id=X when task is claimed returns 409 with error task_claimed and worker_id
DELETE /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}?plan_id=X with valid If-Match returns 204
DELETE /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}?plan_id=X when task is claimed returns 409
PATCH and DELETE without session cookie return 401
PATCH and DELETE with bearer token return 403 (session-only)
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic C (C1 C2 C3) / SC-3.1 SC-3.2 SC-3.3 / §6.1 tasks_api_crud.py / §6.5 API Surface

tasks.description: Implement the 'New Plan' modal in the plans list page: button that opens a modal with plan_id, name, prd_file (optional), budget_usd (optional) inputs. HTMX POSTs to POST /api/v1/plans. On 409 conflict, the plan_id field shows inline 'this plan_id already exists' error. On success, modal closes and navigates to /plans/<new_plan_id>. Modal follows D5 pattern: role=dialog, aria-modal=true, focus on first input, Escape closes. Also implement the plan-row overflow menu (⋯) for Rename, Edit budget, Archive, View JSON — these trigger PATCH /api/v1/plans/{id}.
acceptance_criteria: 'New Plan' button triggers modal with role=dialog and aria-modal=true
Focus moves to plan_id input on modal open
Escape key closes modal and returns focus to 'New Plan' button
Submitting with duplicate plan_id shows inline error 'this plan_id already exists' without page reload
Successful create closes modal and navigates to /plans/<new_plan_id>
Plan row has ⋯ overflow menu with Rename, Edit budget, Archive, View JSON options
Archive sends PATCH with {archived: true}; plan disappears from active list
'Show archived' checkbox reveals archived plans with Restore button
test_steps: python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; html=Path('whilly/api/templates/index.html.j2').read_text(); assert 'role=\"dialog\"' in html or "role='dialog'" in html, 'missing role=dialog'"
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic B (B3 B4 B5) / SC-2.2 / Epic D (D4 D5)

tasks.description: Implement the task Edit modal on /plans/<plan_id>: each task row shows an Edit button for tasks with status in {PENDING, DONE, FAILED, SKIPPED}. Modal (D5 pattern) pre-fills description, priority, key_files, acceptance_criteria, test_steps, dependencies. Save PATCHes /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}?plan_id=X with If-Match header from the task's current ETag. On 412, modal stays open with 'This task changed since you opened the modal. [Reload current values] or [Cancel]'. On 409 (claimed), shows Force-release confirm banner (C5) — two-step: warning + 'Confirm release' red button + grey Cancel.
acceptance_criteria: Edit button is visible only for tasks with status PENDING/DONE/FAILED/SKIPPED
Edit button is hidden (or shows 409 inline) for IN_PROGRESS/CLAIMED tasks
Modal opens with focus on first input field (D5)
Escape closes modal and returns focus to Edit button trigger
On 412: modal stays open with reload/cancel options
On 409 (task_claimed): modal shows two-step Force-release confirm (warning banner + red button + cancel)
Force release POSTs to /tasks/{task_id}/release and closes modal on success
All modal ARIA attributes: role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby pointing to heading
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic C (C1 C2 C5) / SC-3.1 SC-3.3 / Epic D (D5)

tasks.description: Implement task Delete confirmation inline on /plans/<plan_id>: each task row has a ✕ button that shows inline confirm 'Delete task <id>? This cannot be undone. [Confirm] [Cancel]' without a modal (inline expansion or inline replace). Confirm DELETEs /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}?plan_id=X with the current ETag. On 409 (claimed), shows Force-release prompt. On 204 success, the task row is removed from the DOM. Task counts in plan header update.
acceptance_criteria: Delete ✕ button is visible on each task row
Clicking ✕ shows inline confirm 'Delete task <id>? This cannot be undone. [Confirm] [Cancel]'
Clicking Cancel dismisses the confirm without any API call
Clicking Confirm DELETEs the task and removes the row from DOM
On 409 (claimed), show Force-release affordance before allowing delete
Deleted task count updates in the plan summary
test_steps: curl -s -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID' 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/plans/demo' | grep -E 'Delete task|Cannot be undone|Confirm'
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic C (C3) / SC-3.2 / §6.5 DELETE /api/v1/tasks

tasks.description: Write the plans CRUD integration test suite (SC-2.2, SC-3.1, SC-3.2): test_plans_api.py covers POST idempotency (409 on duplicate plan_id), PATCH optimistic concurrency (412 on stale ETag), archive/unarchive roundtrip. test_tasks_api_crud.py covers PATCH 412 on stale version, DELETE audit event payload, claimed-task 409 on both PATCH and DELETE. All tests run against testcontainers Postgres.
acceptance_criteria: test_plans_api.py::test_create_plan_conflict tests 409 on duplicate plan_id
test_plans_api.py::test_patch_plan_stale_etag tests 412
test_plans_api.py::test_archive_unarchive_roundtrip tests archive + unarchive via PATCH
test_tasks_api_crud.py::test_patch_task_stale_etag tests 412 with current ETag in response
test_tasks_api_crud.py::test_delete_task_audit_event checks whilly_events.jsonl for task.deleted payload
test_tasks_api_crud.py::test_claimed_task_blocks_patch_and_delete tests 409 on both
pytest tests/test_plans_api.py tests/test_tasks_api_crud.py -v passes
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-2.2 SC-3.1 SC-3.2 SC-3.3 / §5 Success Criteria

tasks.description: Write the share-link mode regression test (SC-4.1, SC-4.2): verify that GET /?token=<worker-bearer>&plan_id=X (no session cookie) still returns 200 and renders the single-plan dashboard in share-link mode with banner 'You're viewing a shared plan' and no <nav> element. Also verify that after Phase 1+2 changes, the existing dashboard handlers for /api/v1/tasks (GET) and /api/v1/events SSE still work with bearer auth.
acceptance_criteria: GET /?token=WORKER_BEARER&plan_id=X without session cookie returns 200
Response HTML contains 'You\'re viewing a shared plan'
Response HTML does NOT contain <nav (no header nav in share-link mode)
GET /api/v1/tasks?plan_id=X with bearer token returns 200 (existing endpoint unchanged)
SSE endpoint /api/v1/events?plan_id=X with bearer returns 200
pytest tests/test_share_link_regression.py -v passes
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-4.1 SC-4.2 / Epic D (D2) / G3

tasks.description: Configure importlinter CI rule (SC-5.3): add an importlinter contract that fails CI if any module outside whilly.adapters.db.repository references FROM plans or FROM tasks in a raw SQL string. Add the importlinter config to setup.cfg or .importlinter. Add a CI step (Makefile target or GitHub Actions step) that runs importlinter. Write a test that imports importlinter programmatically and checks the contract passes on the current codebase.
acceptance_criteria: importlinter is listed in pyproject.toml dev dependencies (or CI requirements)
setup.cfg or .importlinter defines a contract: only whilly.adapters.db.repository may use raw SQL referencing plans or tasks tables
python -m importlinter passes on the current codebase
Makefile has a lint-imports target or CI step that runs importlinter
Adding a raw FROM plans query outside repository.py causes importlinter to fail (verified by a temporary intentional violation + revert)
test_steps: grep -r 'FROM plans' whilly/ --include='*.py' | grep -v repository.py | grep -v test_ | wc -l | grep '^0$'
grep -r 'FROM tasks' whilly/ --include='*.py' | grep -v repository.py | grep -v test_ | wc -l | grep '^0$'
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-5.3 / Epic B (B7) / Architect F5

tasks.description: Implement session expiry redirect (A7): add a global HTMX event handler that detects htmx:responseError with status 401 and redirects the browser to /login?next=<current-path>, so operators land back on the page they were on after re-authentication. Also implement the 'Signed in as <email>' header element and the logout button (POST /auth/logout with HTMX, then redirect to /login). Handle in-flight loading states on all HTMX-triggered actions: show a spinner or disabled state on the triggering element while the request is in flight.
acceptance_criteria: HTMX 401 response triggers window.location = '/login?next=' + window.location.pathname
Header shows 'Signed in as <email>' when auth_context.email is set
Logout button sends HTMX POST to /auth/logout and redirects to /login
Submit buttons show loading state (disabled + spinner or text change) during HTMX requests
After re-authentication the /login?next= param is used to redirect back to the original page
test_steps: curl -s -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID' http://127.0.0.1:8000/ | grep -E 'Signed in as|logout'
python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; html=Path('whilly/api/templates/index.html.j2').read_text(); assert 'htmx:responseError' in html or 'hx-on' in html, 'missing 401 handler'"
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic A (A7) / Epic D (D3) / Frontend F6 / §5 SC-1.1

tasks.description: Enforce archived plan restrictions: POST /api/v1/tasks?plan_id=X on an archived plan returns 410 Gone (B6). POST /tasks/claim skips archived plans (the claim query filters WHERE archived_at IS NULL). Add integration tests for both behaviors. Also ensure existing /api/v1/tasks GET is unchanged (no include_archived param; tasks are hard-deleted, no soft-delete).
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/tasks?plan_id=ARCHIVED_PLAN returns 410 Gone
POST /tasks/claim with only tasks from archived plans returns 204 or empty (worker idles)
GET /api/v1/tasks is unchanged (no archived filter, no new params)
Workers do not pick up tasks from archived plans (claim query excludes them)
test_steps: psql $DATABASE_URL -c "UPDATE plans SET archived_at=now() WHERE plan_id='test-plan'" && curl -s -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer WORKER_TOKEN' 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/tasks/claim' | python3 -m json.tool
tasks.prd_requirement: Epic B (B6) / SC-5.1 / §4 Story B6

tasks.description: Add ETag headers to existing task and plan GET responses so the task-edit modal can read the ETag for optimistic concurrency. GET /api/v1/tasks (list) should include per-task ETag in JSON payload (as a field, since it's a list). GET /api/v1/plans should include per-plan ETag. The ETag value should be a quoted string of the entity's current version number (e.g., '"42"'). This is needed for If-Match on PATCH/DELETE.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/plans response includes ETag per plan in JSON payload as 'version' or 'etag' field
GET /api/v1/tasks response includes per-task version field
PATCH /api/v1/tasks/{id} response has ETag header with new version
PATCH /api/v1/plans/{id} response has ETag header with new version
ETag format is quoted string: '"<version>"'
test_steps: curl -s -H 'Cookie: whilly_session=VALID' http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/plans | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['plans'][0].get('version','MISSING'))"
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-3.1 / Epic C (C2) / Architect F6 / §6.5 API Surface

tasks.description: Write snapshot tests for the rendered HTML of share-link mode (SC-4.2) and the login-consumed page (A5). These capture the exact HTML structure so regressions in template changes are caught. Use pytest + requests or httpx against the test server. Snapshot files committed to tests/snapshots/.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_template_snapshots.py captures share-link mode HTML to tests/snapshots/share_link_mode.html
tests/test_template_snapshots.py captures login_consumed.html.j2 render to tests/snapshots/login_consumed.html
Snapshots are committed and checked against on subsequent runs
pytest tests/test_template_snapshots.py -v passes
tasks.prd_requirement: SC-4.2 / Epic A (A5) / §5 Success Criteria

tasks.description: Full acceptance demo smoke test: implement a pytest test (or Makefile target) that executes Appendix B steps 1–8 programmatically using httpx against a locally running server + fresh DB: fresh DB via alembic upgrade head, POST /auth/login, parse magic link from whilly_events.jsonl, GET /auth/magic, verify cookie set, GET /api/v1/plans (empty), POST /api/v1/plans (demo), GET /plans/demo, re-use consumed magic link and verify login_consumed page. Steps 9–18 can remain as a manual checklist in the PR description.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_acceptance_demo.py implements steps 1–8 of Appendix B
Test uses a real DB (testcontainers) and real server (pytest-anyio or subprocess)
Step 3: GET / without cookie → 302 to /login
Step 5–6: POST /auth/login → parse link from jsonl → GET /auth/magic → Set-Cookie present
Step 7: GET /api/v1/plans returns empty list + 'Create your first plan' marker
Step 8: reuse consumed token → login_consumed page rendered
pytest tests/test_acceptance_demo.py -v passes
tasks.prd_requirement: Appendix B / SC-1.1 / §8 Phase 2 Exit Criteria
